sections.title: Standard Equipment
sections.description: Specifications for carrom board, carrommen, striker, and accessories

rules.title: Playing Surface
rules.text: The playing surface of the Carrom Board should be of plywood or of any other wood equally smooth and be of not less than 8 mm in thickness and of a minimum of 73.50 cm and a maximum of 74 cm square with its surface completely smooth.

rules.title: Frames
rules.text: Wooden frames with inside curved corners made of Rosewood or any other hard wood, minimum height 1.90 cm and maximum 2.54 cm from the playing surface. Breadth not less than 6.35 cm and not more than 7.60 cm.

rules.title: Pockets
rules.text: The pockets of the four corners shall be round inwardly, 4.45 cm in diameter with admissible variation of not more than 0.15 cm.

rules.title: Base Lines
rules.text: Two straight lines of 47 cm in length, black in colour, drawn on each of the four sides parallel to the frame. Lower line is 10.15 cm from frame, upper line 3.18 cm from lower line. Base circles of 3.18 cm diameter at either end with red center of 2.54 cm.

rules.title: Arrows
rules.text: Four black arrows at each corner at 45 degrees, passing through the gap between Base circles, pointing toward pocket center, leaving 5.00 cm clearance from pocket edge. Maximum length 25.70 cm.

rules.title: Centre Circle
rules.text: Exactly in the centre of the Carrom Board there shall be a circle of 3.18 cm in diameter drawn black in colour, coloured red.

rules.title: Outer Circle
rules.text: A circle of 17.00 cm in diameter with the centre point as its centre, drawn black with admissible variation of 0.30 cm.

rules.title: Carrommen
rules.text: Good quality wood, circular shape. Diameter 3.02 to 3.18 cm. Thickness 0.70 to 0.90 cm. Weight 5.00 to 5.50 grams. Nine whites, nine blacks, one red Queen.

rules.title: Striker
rules.text: Smooth and round, diameter not more than 4.13 cm, weighing not more than 15.00 gms. No metal visible. Engraved decent designs permissible.

rules.title: Table or Stand
rules.text: Height between 63.00 cm and 70.00 cm. Board must not be uneven or shaking when placed on it.

rules.title: Stool or Chair
rules.text: Height between 40.00 cm and 50.00 cm. If chair is used, it shall be armless.

rules.title: Powder
rules.text: High quality powder to keep playing surface smooth and dry. Not wet. Spread evenly using pouches/containers.

rules.title: Nets
rules.text: Pockets shall be fully covered from beneath by nets fixed at bottom. Each net shall hold at least 10 C/m.

rules.title: Light
rules.text: Hung at convenient height with conical/square shade (preferably metal). Inner shade snow-white. Bulb 60-100 watts, preferably transparent white. Adjustable on player request.

sections.title: Interpretations
sections.description: Definitions of terms used throughout the Laws

rules.title: Laws
rules.text: 'Laws' shall mean the Laws of Carrom.

rules.title: Proper
rules.text: 'Proper' shall mean in accordance with the Laws.

rules.title: Improper
rules.text: 'Improper' shall mean contrary to the Laws.

rules.title: C/B
rules.text: 'C/B' shall mean Carrom Board.

rules.title: C/m
rules.text: 'C/m' shall mean Carromman / Carrommen.

rules.title: Board
rules.text: 'Board' shall mean break to completion.

rules.title: Break
rules.text: 'Break' shall mean the first stroke of a board.

rules.title: Finish
rules.text: 'Finish' shall mean the completion of the board.

rules.title: Player
rules.text: 'Player' shall mean a Carrom player.

rules.title: Placing
rules.text: 'Placing' shall mean keeping the penalty and/or Due C/m in a flat position within the Outer circle only by the player permitted to do so. Queen and/or jumped C/m always placed by Umpire in Centre Circle.

rules.title: Pocketing
rules.text: 'Pocketing' shall mean putting C/m and/or Queen in the pocket by a proper/improper stroke.

rules.title: Push
rules.text: 'Push' shall mean a jerk or sudden motion of the elbow of the playing hand instead of striking the Striker with the tip of the finger.

rules.title: Queen
rules.text: 'Queen' shall mean the red C/m.

rules.title: Due
rules.text: 'Due' shall mean pocketing the Striker with or without C/m.

rules.title: Penalty
rules.text: 'Penalty' shall mean punishment for infringement or violation of the Laws.

rules.title: Covering
rules.text: 'Covering' shall mean pocketing one of his own C/m by a player in the same or immediate subsequent stroke, when or after the Queen is pocketed.

rules.title: Shot
rules.text: 'Shot' shall mean a pair or cannon.

rules.title: Pair
rules.text: Placing a C/m within the Outer Circle near another C/m and/or Queen with space between, where the existing C/m faces the general direction of a pocket.

rules.title: Cannon
rules.text: Placing a C/m within the Outer Circle near another C/m and/or Queen with no space between, where the existing C/m faces the general direction of a pocket.

rules.title: Thumbing
rules.text: 'Thumbing' shall mean taking a stroke with the thumb.

rules.title: Turn
rules.text: 'Turn' shall mean the right to strike.

rules.title: Chief Referee
rules.text: 'Chief Referee' shall mean an official appointed to supervise and/or control a match.

rules.title: Umpire
rules.text: 'Umpire' shall mean an official appointed to supervise and/or control a match.

rules.title: Opponent in Singles
rules.text: The player presently not having his turn of play.

rules.title: Opponent in Doubles
rules.text: The player sitting on the left and/or right side of the player presently having his turn to play.

rules.title: Hand
rules.text: 'Hand' shall mean the portion of the playing hand from the fingers up to the wrist.

rules.title: Finger
rules.text: 'Finger' shall mean the portion of the nail side of a finger up to the second joint.

rules.title: Imaginary Lines
rules.text: Lines drawn in extension of the arrows between the Base Circles.

rules.title: Stroke
rules.text: 'Stroke' shall mean hitting the C/m by the Striker directly or indirectly.

rules.title: White Slam
rules.text: Pocketing all nine white C/m and the Queen as per Laws in the first turn of play. Also called 'Break to Finish'.

rules.title: Black Slam
rules.text: Pocketing all the remaining black C/m with or without Queen as per Laws in the first turn of play.

sections.title: Sitting Position
sections.description: Rules for player seating during play

rules.title: Singles Sitting
rules.text: In Singles, the players shall sit opposite to each other.

rules.title: Doubles Sitting
rules.text: In Doubles, the partners shall sit opposite to each other, occupying all four sides.

rules.title: Position Changes
rules.text: Position adopted before turn may be changed at any time provided the chair/stool is not lifted, moved, or disturbed during turn of play.

rules.title: Body Contact
rules.text: During the board no part of the body, except the playing arm, shall touch the Carrom Board, stand or table.

rules.title: Clothing Exception
rules.text: Wearing/clothing, ring, bangles, watch worn by a player are exempted during turn, but should not touch the playing surface.

rules.title: Seat Adjustment
rules.text: Use of any material to raise or adjust the height of the seat is permissible only after completion of the board.

rules.title: Body Beyond Lines
rules.text: No part of the body, except the hand of the player, shall go beyond the imaginary lines of the arrows.

sections.title: How to Strike
sections.description: Rules for the proper striking technique

rules.title: Strike Not Push
rules.text: The Striker shall be struck and not pushed.

rules.title: Finger Use
rules.text: The Stroke shall be made with the finger with or without support of other fingers.

rules.title: Either Hand
rules.text: Any hand may be used in play.

rules.title: Hand on Surface
rules.text: While taking the stroke, the hand may touch the playing surface.

rules.title: Elbow Position
rules.text: The elbow of the playing hand shall not come within the playing surface nor extend beyond the imaginary lines of the arrows.

rules.title: Hand Crossing
rules.text: The hand may cross the arrow.

rules.title: No External Support
rules.text: Taking support of Stool/Chair, Stand/Table, or keeping legs on rim of stand/table is not permissible.

rules.title: Body Rest Allowed
rules.text: Hands may rest on his body, and legs may rest on the rim of the stool or chair on which he sits.

sections.title: Toss
sections.description: Coin toss procedure at start of match

rules.title: Toss Procedure
rules.text: Umpire conducts toss by coin spin or calling C/m. Winner chooses side or option to strike first.

rules.title: Doubles Toss
rules.text: In Doubles, the pair winning the toss shall have the option as above.

rules.title: Side Choice
rules.text: If break is chosen by winner, choice of side lies with losers, and winner shall sit first.

rules.title: No Interchange
rules.text: Once losers have sat down, they cannot interchange. Order of sitting continues throughout match.

sections.title: Trial Board
sections.description: Practice rounds before the match begins

rules.title: Trial Boards
rules.text: There shall be two trial boards only after the toss and before the start of the match, one for each player or pair.

sections.title: Break
sections.description: Rules for arranging carrommen and the opening stroke

rules.title: Arrangement Pattern
rules.text: Queen in Centre Circle. First row: black and white C/m alternately around Queen. Second row: three white C/m form 'Y' shape with white C/m in first row. Remaining filled alternately. All in compact round, touching each other, within Outer Circle.

rules.title: Quick Arrangement
rules.text: C/m should be arranged for the break with least possible loss of time after each board.

rules.title: Who Breaks
rules.text: Break is taken by a player who has chosen to strike first.

rules.title: Color Assignment
rules.text: Player who breaks has white C/m. Opponent has black C/m. Queen is common.

rules.title: Valid Break
rules.text: Break is considered to have been made if the Striker touches any of the C/m even slightly.

rules.title: Failed Break Chances
rules.text: If no C/m touched, maximum two more chances allowed.

rules.title: Lost Break Right
rules.text: After permissible chances with no C/m touched, right to break is lost. Turn passes to opponent who has black C/m. No re-arrangement permitted.

rules.title: Improper Break
rules.text: If improper stroke or Striker pocketed without touching C/m during break, player loses turn. No Due/penalty applicable.

rules.title: Break Timing
rules.text: Break taken only after Umpire calls 'Play'. Stroke within 15 seconds of call.

rules.title: Premature Break
rules.text: If break made before 'Play' called, all pocketed C/m and/or Queen taken out. Additional C/m as penalty. Due declared. Player loses turn.

sections.title: Turn of Play
sections.description: Rules governing whose turn it is and when

rules.title: Continuing Turn
rules.text: As long as a player pockets his own C/m and/or Queen per Laws, his turn continues. Otherwise it passes to opponent.

rules.title: First Game Break
rules.text: Player who chooses to break first board has white C/m. Turn to break passes alternately during the game.

rules.title: Second Game Break
rules.text: In second game, player who did not have first turn to break has his turn first.

rules.title: Third Game Break
rules.text: In third game, turn to break passes to first player.

rules.title: Doubles Turn Order
rules.text: In Doubles, turn passes to player sitting to right hand side of player who had his turn.

rules.title: 15-Second Limit
rules.text: Player shall not take more than 15 seconds for making a stroke from the moment the C/m/Queen/Striker has come to rest and picked up by opponent.

rules.title: Out-of-Turn Play
rules.text: If turn availed out of turn before Umpire could control, offending player loses board by number of C/m and Queen on C/B. If unnoticed till next stroke, turn allowed and next turn as per Laws.

rules.title: Winning Board
rules.text: The player who completes pocketing all his C/m first wins the board.

rules.title: Point Values
rules.text: Queen: 3 points up to and including 21 points. C/m: 1 point each.

rules.title: Calculating Points
rules.text: Number of C/m of opponent on C/B is points gained by player in that board.

rules.title: Queen Credit
rules.text: Player credited with Queen value only if he wins the board.

rules.title: Loser No Queen
rules.text: Player who loses board not credited with Queen value, even if pocketed and covered Queen.

rules.title: 22-Point Threshold
rules.text: Player loses advantage of additional 3 points for covering Queen once he reaches 22 points.

rules.title: Maximum Per Board
rules.text: Maximum points scorable in a board is 12. Any Due/penalty C/m automatically written off.

rules.title: Game Length
rules.text: A game is 25 points or eight boards. Player reaching 25 first or leading at end of 8th board wins.

rules.title: Tiebreaker
rules.text: Up to pre-quarter finals, equal score at end of 8th board means extra board. Toss before extra board for break only.

rules.title: Match Format
rules.text: All matches decided by best of three games only.

sections.title: Change of Sides
sections.description: When and how players switch positions

rules.title: Singles Change
rules.text: In Singles, change of sides made in opposite direction at conclusion of each game.

rules.title: Doubles Change
rules.text: In Doubles, change of sides made to next right hand side at conclusion of each game.

rules.title: Third Game Pre-QF
rules.text: In third game for matches up to pre-quarter finals, change of sides after fourth board or when any player/pair reaches 13 points, whichever earlier.

rules.title: Quarter Finals Onward
rules.text: From Quarter Finals onwards, change of sides only after 13 points scored by any player/pair.

rules.title: Unnoticed Change
rules.text: Change of sides remaining unnoticed by Umpire or player takes place when noticed, but after completion of that board.

rules.title: Two-Minute Limit
rules.text: Players shall not take more than two minutes to change sides.

rules.title: Foul Categories
rules.text: Any violation of Laws is broadly classified as: (a) Technical Foul, (b) Foul.

rules.title: Technical Foul - Before Stroke
rules.text: Violation by player BEFORE first stroke of his turn = Technical Foul. One C/m of offending player brought out for placing by opponent. Turn continues.

rules.title: Technical Foul - Not Playing
rules.text: Violation by player presently NOT having his turn = Technical Foul. Penalty per Rule 63(a).

rules.title: Foul - During Turn
rules.text: Violation DURING or AFTER first stroke of turn = Foul. One C/m brought out for placing. Turn lost.

rules.title: Foul While Pocketing
rules.text: If foul committed while pocketing C/m/Queen, the pocketed C/m/Queen brought out for placing. Turn lost.

sections.title: C/m Overboard
sections.description: Rules when carrommen leave the playing surface

rules.title: Jumped C/m Placement
rules.text: If C/m/Queen jumps out, Umpire places it in Centre Circle if space permits, covering it fully or maximum available portion.

rules.title: Queen + C/m Jump
rules.text: If both Queen and C/m jump in same stroke, Queen placed first. Jumped C/m placed touching Queen, opposite direction of player having turn.

rules.title: Both Colors Jump
rules.text: If both white and black C/m jump in same stroke, C/m of player who made stroke placed first. Other touching first, in manner of 65(b).

rules.title: Multiple C/m Jump
rules.text: If more than 2 C/m jump in same stroke, first 2 placed per 65(b)/(c). Rest placed touching first 2, as far as possible.

rules.title: Fall Back on Surface
rules.text: If C/m/Queen jumps and falls back on playing surface, Umpire places in Centre Circle. Position of disturbed C/m corrected by Umpire at his discretion.

rules.title: Light Fitting Hit
rules.text: If C/m falls back after hitting shade/bulb/light fittings, considered to have naturally traveled. Disturbed C/m not rearranged.

rules.title: Standing on Rim
rules.text: If C/m/Queen stands on its rim, allowed to remain as is.

rules.title: Overlapping C/m
rules.text: If two C/m/Queen overlap each other, left undisturbed.

rules.title: Striker on C/m
rules.text: If Striker rests on C/m/Queen, Umpire removes Striker without disturbance. If disturbed, original position restored as far as possible.

rules.title: Pocket Mouth Fall
rules.text: If at pocket mouth and C/m loses center of gravity during Striker removal and falls, deemed pocketed.

rules.title: C/m on Striker
rules.text: If C/m/Queen rests on Striker, Umpire lifts C/m, replaces them as far as possible in position they would rest if Striker absent.

rules.title: Striker Falls In
rules.text: If at pocket mouth and Striker loses center of gravity during C/m removal and falls in, deemed pocketed. Penalty declared per Laws.

rules.title: Precarious Pocket Fall
rules.text: If C/m resting precariously at mouth of pocket falls in for any reason, considered properly pocketed.

sections.title: Dues and/or Penalties
sections.description: Rules for penalty carrommen and Due C/m

rules.title: Striker Alone Pocketed
rules.text: If proper/improper stroke pockets Striker alone, turn lost. One C/m taken out as penalty (Due) by opponent for placing.

rules.title: Outstanding Due
rules.text: If happens before any of his C/m pocketed, Due remains outstanding until C/m available.

rules.title: Striker + Own C/m
rules.text: If pockets Striker with own C/m: pocketed C/m + Due C/m taken out for placing. Turn continues.

rules.title: Striker + Opponent C/m
rules.text: If pockets Striker with opponent's C/m: opponent's C/m deemed pocketed. Due taken out for placing. Turn lost.

rules.title: Striker + Both C/m
rules.text: If pockets Striker with own and opponent's C/m: own C/m + Due taken out for placing by opponent. Turn continues.

rules.title: Improper Opponent C/m
rules.text: If pockets opponent's C/m by improper stroke: C/m deemed pocketed. Due taken out. Turn lost.

rules.title: Improper Own C/m
rules.text: If pockets own C/m by improper stroke: C/m + Due taken out for placing. Turn lost.

rules.title: Improper Striker + Own
rules.text: If pockets own C/m with Striker by improper stroke: C/m + Due taken out. Turn lost.

rules.title: Placing Time
rules.text: Due/penalty C/m placed immediately after available, but only after stroke conclusion.

rules.title: Doubles Due Placing
rules.text: In Doubles, Due/penalty always placed by player sitting on right hand side of player having turn at time of availability.

rules.title: Same Player Places
rules.text: If during turn player pockets opponent's C/m and Due becomes available, the player himself takes out C/m for placing.

rules.title: No Space for Placing
rules.text: If Due/penalty available but no space, player permitted to place immediately after space available.

rules.title: Doubles Eligibility Pass
rules.text: In Doubles, if eligibility passed to partner, only partner has right to take out and place C/m.

rules.title: Forfeit Placing
rules.text: If space available but player doesn't want to risk foul, loses chance to place. Claim forfeited.

rules.title: Mid-Turn Space
rules.text: If space becomes available during turn of eligible player, placing made immediately.

rules.title: Wrong C/m Placed
rules.text: If player places own C/m by mistake, must be rectified if pointed out. Foul declared.

rules.title: Unnoticed Wrong Placement
rules.text: If not noticed before next stroke, C/m placed regarded as valid.

rules.title: Multiple Dues
rules.text: If more than one Due/penalty C/m to be placed: available C/m placed immediately, rest as soon as available.

rules.title: Placing Complete
rules.text: Placing complete once finger removed from C/m, provided C/m placed inside Outer Circle.

rules.title: No Other C/m
rules.text: While placing Due/penalty, holding any other C/m or Striker not permitted.

rules.title: Placed Outside Circle
rules.text: If player places/moves Due/penalty outside Outer Circle, asked to rectify. Foul declared.

rules.title: Disturbing Other C/m
rules.text: While placing, must not move other C/m/Queen. If happens, replaced by Umpire. Foul declared.

rules.title: Forego Due
rules.text: Player may forego Due/penalty in Toto only, not partially. Decide within 15 seconds or right forfeited.

rules.title: 15-Second Placing
rules.text: Time limit for placing Due/penalty is 15 seconds after Umpire's announcement.

rules.title: No Set Off
rules.text: Due/penalty C/m cannot be set off against each other.

rules.title: Centre Circle Coverage
rules.text: Due/penalty not placed covering Centre Circle wholly or partially. Must rectify. Foul declared.

rules.title: Doubles Partner Takeout
rules.text: In Doubles, player shall not take out penalty/Due C/m for partner. Request Umpire if needed colour/number not available.

rules.title: Getting Up
rules.text: If player gets up during turn or opponent's turn for any reason, loses board with number of C/m/Queen on board. If opponent's score 22+, loses by C/m only.

sections.title: Queen
sections.description: Comprehensive rules for the red Queen carromman

rules.title: Right to Pocket Queen
rules.text: Player has right to pocket Queen and cover it provided one of his own C/m already pocketed.

rules.title: Queen Placement
rules.text: Queen placed by Umpire only in Centre Circle. If shot automatically formed during placement, cannot be altered.

rules.title: Centre Circle Covered
rules.text: If Centre Circle partially/completely covered, Queen placed in most uncovered portion or adjacent position making it not easy to pocket. Umpire's placement final.

rules.title: Queen Before First C/m
rules.text: If Queen pocketed before any of player's C/m, Queen taken out for placing. Turn lost.

rules.title: Queen with Due
rules.text: If pockets Queen while Due against him, Queen taken out for placing. Turn lost.

rules.title: Recovery After Due
rules.text: After recovery of Due/penalty, if all nine C/m on C/B, player has right to pocket Queen and cover it.

rules.title: Queen + Striker at Break
rules.text: At break or subsequent stroke when all nine C/m on C/B, if Queen pocketed with Striker: Queen taken out for placing, Due declared, turn lost.

rules.title: Queen Not Covered
rules.text: If Queen pocketed but not covered, taken out for placing. If unnoticed before next stroke, recorded as properly covered.

rules.title: Queen + Own C/m
rules.text: If Queen and own C/m pocketed together in one stroke, Queen considered covered.

rules.title: Break with All 9 C/m
rules.text: At break/subsequent stroke when all 9 C/m on board, if Queen + 1 C/m pocketed: Queen must be covered. If more than 1 C/m + Queen pocketed: Queen covered.

rules.title: Queen + C/m + Striker (Proper)
rules.text: If Queen, C/m, Striker pocketed together by proper stroke: Queen + C/m + 1 additional Due taken out for placing. Turn continues.

rules.title: Queen + C/m + Striker (Improper)
rules.text: If Queen, C/m, Striker pocketed by improper stroke: Queen + C/m + 2 additional Due taken out. Turn lost.

rules.title: Queen + Striker (Proper)
rules.text: If Queen and Striker pocketed by proper stroke: Queen taken out by Umpire. 1 additional C/m taken out for placing. Turn continues.

rules.title: Queen + Striker (Improper)
rules.text: If Queen and Striker pocketed by improper stroke: Queen taken out by Umpire. 2 additional C/m taken out for placing. Turn lost.

rules.title: Covering: Striker Alone
rules.text: While covering Queen, if Striker alone pocketed: Queen taken out for placing. 1 C/m of offending player taken out as Due. Turn lost.

rules.title: Covering: Striker (Improper)
rules.text: While covering Queen, if Striker alone pocketed by improper stroke: Queen taken out. 2 C/m taken out as Due. Turn lost.

rules.title: Covering: Striker + C/m
rules.text: While covering Queen, if Striker + own C/m pocketed: C/m + 1 Due taken out for placing. Turn continues. If subsequent stroke fails to pocket C/m, Queen not considered covered, taken out for placing.

rules.title: Covering: Striker + C/m (Improper)
rules.text: While covering Queen, if Striker + own C/m pocketed by improper stroke: C/m + 2 Due taken out. Turn lost.

rules.title: Covering: Last + Last (Proper)
rules.text: While covering Queen, if pockets last own C/m + last opponent C/m: awarded 3 points. If score 22+, wins by 1 point.

rules.title: Covering: Last + Last (Improper)
rules.text: While covering Queen, if pockets last own + last opponent C/m by improper stroke: opponent wins by 3 points. If score 22+, wins by 1 point. If demanded, additional point for improper stroke awarded opponent.

rules.title: Covering: Last Opponent Only
rules.text: While covering Queen, if pockets last opponent C/m: loses board by number of own C/m + Queen value. If opponent score 22+, loses by C/m only.

rules.title: Covering: Last Opp (Improper)
rules.text: While covering Queen, if pockets last opponent C/m by improper stroke: same as 103a. If demanded, additional point awarded opponent.

rules.title: Queen + Last + Last (Proper)
rules.text: If pockets Queen with last own C/m and last opponent C/m by proper stroke: wins board by 3 points. If score 22+, wins by 1.

rules.title: Queen + Last + Last (Improper)
rules.text: If pockets Queen with last own + last opponent by improper stroke: opponent wins by 3 points. If score 22+, loses by 1. If demanded, 1 additional point awarded.

rules.title: Last + Last with Queen on Board
rules.text: If pockets last own + last opponent by proper stroke while Queen on C/B: opponent awarded 3 points. If score 22+, awarded 1 point.

rules.title: Last + Last (Improper) Queen on Board
rules.text: Same as 105a but improper stroke: opponent awarded 3 points. If 22+, 1 point. If demanded, opponent awarded 1 additional point.

rules.title: Last Opp Only with Queen on Board
rules.text: If pockets last opponent C/m by proper stroke when Queen still on C/B: loses board by number of own C/m + Queen value. If opponent 22+, loses by C/m only.

rules.title: Last Opp Only (Improper) Queen on Board
rules.text: Same as 106a but improper: same loss. If demanded, opponent awarded 1 additional point.

rules.title: Last Own with Queen on Board
rules.text: If pockets last own C/m by proper stroke leaving Queen on C/B: loses board by 3 points. If opponent 22+, loses by 1 point.

rules.title: Last Own (Improper) Queen on Board
rules.text: Same as 107a but improper: 3 points loss. If 22+, 1 point. If demanded, opponent awarded 1 additional point.

rules.title: Last Own + Striker, Queen on Board
rules.text: If pockets last own C/m with Striker leaving Queen by proper stroke: loses board by 3 points. If opponent 22+, loses by 1. If demanded, 1 additional for Striker.

rules.title: Last Own + Striker (Improper)
rules.text: Same as 108a but improper: 3 points loss. If 22+, 1. If demanded, 2 additional points.

rules.title: Queen + Last + Last + Striker (Proper)
rules.text: If pockets Queen + last own + last opponent + Striker by proper stroke: loses board by 3 points. If 22+, 1. If demanded, 1 additional for Striker.

rules.title: Queen + Last + Last + Striker (Improper)
rules.text: Same as 109a but improper: 3 points loss. If 22+, 1. If demanded, 2 additional points.

rules.title: Last + Last + Striker (Queen Covered)
rules.text: If pockets last own + last opponent with Striker by proper stroke: loses board by 1 point if Queen covered by him. If demanded, 1 additional for Striker.

rules.title: Last + Last + Striker (Improper, Queen Covered)
rules.text: Same as 110a but improper: loses by 1 point if Queen covered. If demanded, 2 additional points.

rules.title: Last Opp + Striker, Queen on Board (Proper)
rules.text: If pockets last opponent + Striker leaving Queen by proper stroke: loses by number of own C/m + Queen value. If 22+, by C/m only. If demanded, 1 additional for Striker.

rules.title: Last Opp + Striker (Improper)
rules.text: Same as 111a but improper: same loss. If demanded, 2 additional points.

rules.title: Last + Last + Striker (Opp Covered Queen)
rules.text: If pockets last own + last opponent with Striker by proper stroke: loses by 3 points if Queen covered by opponent. If demanded, 1 additional point.

rules.title: Last + Last + Striker (Improper, Opp Covered)
rules.text: Same as 112a but improper: 3 points loss if Queen covered by opponent. If demanded, 2 additional points.

rules.title: Queen Falls In
rules.text: If Queen resting precariously at pocket mouth falls in for any reason, considered duly pocketed.

rules.title: Due for Queen Shot
rules.text: Player shall not utilize Due/penalty C/m to make shot with Queen. If utilized, Umpire asks rectification. Foul declared.

sections.title: General
sections.description: Miscellaneous rules covering equipment use, timing, and conduct

rules.title: Striker Source
rules.text: Stroke taken by means of Striker only. Brought by player and certified by Chief Referee/Umpire before play.

rules.title: Striker Jump
rules.text: If Striker jumps over C/B during stroke, turn continues if player pockets own C/m/Queen in that stroke.

rules.title: Changed Striker
rules.text: Changed new Striker, duly approved, allowed after particular board completed. If Striker breaks mid-board, change permitted only after current stroke completed.

rules.title: C/B Position
rules.text: Once set, position of C/B not changed in middle of match.

rules.title: Umpire Authority
rules.text: Sole authority to make changes after examination, on appeal by player, lies with Umpire.

rules.title: C/B Change
rules.text: C/B, table, or stand can be changed only after completion of board. Sole authority for deciding condition rests with Umpire.

rules.title: Broken C/m
rules.text: Broken C/m, if appealed for replacement, replaced by Umpire in original position as far as possible.

rules.title: Time Stop on Appeal
rules.text: Time count stops when appeal made to Umpire during play. Play not continues unless Umpire orders 'Play'.

rules.title: Violation Loss
rules.text: Player who violates loses entire board with number of C/m on C/B + Queen points if on C/B.

rules.title: Powder Application
rules.text: Powder applied evenly by player having turn over playing surface before break. Neither excessive nor scanty.

rules.title: Powder Timing
rules.text: Powder not applied or removed after Umpire announces 'Play'.

rules.title: Willful Removal
rules.text: Any willful attempt to remove or wipe powder from playing surface not permitted.

rules.title: Removal Methods
rules.text: Removal of powder by blowing, wiping, or any other way not permitted.

rules.title: Base Lines Powder
rules.text: Excessive powder along Base Lines/Circles of his side may be removed with Striker only, by player during turn.

rules.title: Umpire Powder Control
rules.text: Umpire has right to control even application of powder on playing surface.

rules.title: Dust Removal Request
rules.text: Umpires should be requested for removal of dust, insects, etc., from C/B during play.

rules.title: Stroke Completion
rules.text: Stroke considered complete only after Striker, C/m/Queen disturbed by Striker comes to rest. Striker removed by player immediately. Umpire may assist on request.

rules.title: Time Start (Pocketed)
rules.text: When player pockets own C/m/Queen, time count starts as soon as stroke complete.

rules.title: Time Start (Failed)
rules.text: When player fails to pocket, time count starts only after Striker removed from C/B.

rules.title: Opponent C/m Pocketed
rules.text: When player pockets opponent's C/m knowingly or unknowingly, Umpire stops player from playing further.

rules.title: Unnoticed Opp C/m
rules.text: If unnoticed by Umpire/opponent, C/m considered properly pocketed. Turn lost when noticed.

rules.title: Hit/Tap Board
rules.text: Player shall not hit, tap, or disturb C/B willfully or otherwise. If done, foul declared.

rules.title: Major Disturbance
rules.text: If done in way rearrangement becomes impossible, loses entire board with C/m + Queen points per Laws.

rules.title: Striker Slips
rules.text: Stroke considered made if Striker slips, leaving any Base Line/Circle, regardless of touching C/m.

rules.title: Slip No Movement
rules.text: If Striker slips and doesn't leave Base Line/Circle or move any C/m, stroke not considered made.

rules.title: Score Inquiry
rules.text: Player may find out score from Umpire only during his turn of play.

rules.title: No Distraction
rules.text: Player shall not distract attention of opponent by any act.

rules.title: Striker on Base Lines
rules.text: While taking stroke, Striker must touch both Base Lines.

rules.title: Base Circle Stroke
rules.text: If stroke from Base Circle, Striker must cover Base Circle fully but not touch arrow.

rules.title: No Doubles Communication
rules.text: In Doubles, partners not allowed to talk or communicate by gestures during board. Violation = Foul.

rules.title: No Spectator Talk
rules.text: Players shall not talk to spectators during course of game without Umpire permission.

rules.title: No Solid Material
rules.text: Player shall not hold any solid material except Striker in either hand during turn.

rules.title: C/m Disturbance After Break
rules.text: C/m/Queen not moved or disturbed after break except by stroke. If touched/moved/disturbed, replaced by Umpire. Foul declared.

rules.title: No Run Test
rules.text: Run of Striker not tested on playing surface during course of board.

rules.title: Striker on Frame
rules.text: Player shall not keep Striker on frame or in pocket of C/B during board.

rules.title: Striker Near Base
rules.text: Player permitted to place Striker/C/m on playing surface, near his Base Line only, while placing Due/penalty C/m.

rules.title: Three Pass
rules.text: If players pass turn consecutively 3 times each, that board cancelled and replayed.

rules.title: Game Interval
rules.text: 10 minutes interval between second and third game.

rules.title: Concession
rules.text: Player permitted to concede game/match at any stage.

rules.title: Unforeseen Circumstances
rules.text: If Umpire feels rearrangement of C/m not possible due to unforeseen circumstances, that board replayed.

rules.title: Broken C/m Position
rules.text: If C/m broken by stroke during board, position of bigger part considered for all purposes. Umpire's decision final.

rules.title: C/m Blocking Striker
rules.text: If C/m on Baseline/Circle prevents player from placing Striker, that board replayed.

sections.title: Loss of Entire Match
sections.description: Acts that cause forfeiture of the entire match

rules.title: Leaves Match Area
rules.text: Leaves match board area during game without Umpire permission.

rules.title: Time Limit Failure
rules.text: Fails to abide by time limit for rest in deciding game.

rules.title: Refuses Decision
rules.text: Refuses to abide by decision of Umpire/Chief Referee.

rules.title: Insulting Remarks
rules.text: Passes insulting remarks against Chief Referee/Umpire/authorities before, during, or after match.

rules.title: Intoxication
rules.text: Smokes or smells foul while at play due to intoxicating drinks, or under influence of drugs.

rules.title: Uncertified Striker
rules.text: Plays with an uncertified Striker.

rules.title: Discontinues Play
rules.text: Discontinues play during course of match without Umpire permission.

rules.title: Distraction Repeated
rules.text: Disturbs and distracts attention of opponent more than twice despite Umpire's warning.

rules.title: Late to Match
rules.text: Fails to report to Umpire at match board within 15 minutes after match announced.

sections.title: Protest
sections.description: Procedures for filing and resolving protests

rules.title: Written Protest
rules.text: All protests written in English addressed to Secretary of Tournament. Submitted through Chief Referee/Referee duly signed by player/Captain/Manager.

rules.title: Oral Protest
rules.text: Oral protest intimated to Umpire immediately when incident occurs, with prescribed scrutiny fee. No protest entertained without payment.

rules.title: Protest Time Limit
rules.text: Written protest submitted within 15 minutes of completion of that particular board.

rules.title: Protest Content
rules.text: Protest represents facts only as occurred, decision of Chief Referee/Umpire, and point appealed against.

rules.title: Abusive Protest
rules.text: Protest in uncouth and abusive language summarily rejected. Invites disciplinary action.

rules.title: No Withdrawal
rules.text: Once protest lodged, not permitted to be withdrawn.

rules.title: Continue Play
rules.text: Once protest recorded and handed over, play continues from where stopped. Result not announced until protest considered.

rules.title: Protest Upheld
rules.text: If protest upheld, match continues from beginning of that particular board where protest lodged. Scrutiny fee refunded.

rules.title: Protest Overruled
rules.text: If protest overruled, result already obtained stands. Scrutiny fee forfeited.

rules.title: Decision Time
rules.text: Decision of Tournament authorities conveyed to parties within one hour from time protest lodged.
